resposta:
solution:
```
class Solution:
    def canMakeSubsequence(self, str1: str, str2: str) -> bool:
        m, n = len(str1), len(str2)
        i, j = 0, 0
        while i < m and j < n:
            if str1[i] == str2[j]:
                j += 1
            i += 1
        if j == n:
            return True
        i, j = m - 1, n - 1
        while i >= 0 and j >= 0:
            if str1[i] == str2[j]:
                j -= 1
            i -= 1
        return j < 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(m + n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses two pointers to check if str2 is a subsequence of str1 and its cyclically shifted versions.
